coverage_summary: The three active departments (marketing, finance, operations) together cover the full business scope implied by the orchestrator charter and the founder's priorities. Marketing owns outward-facing narrative and mailing-list audience development. Operations owns the physical and regulatory flow of wine from vineyard to customer, including TTB/COLA compliance. Finance owns the money layer: books, tax compliance, financing, and the controller function gating committed spend. One explicit overlap was resolved (label copy review) with a named handoff. No gaps were identified; no recommendation to add a fourth department at this time.

overlaps_resolved.resolution: Marketing drafts label copy in the brand voice; operations owns the TTB/COLA compliance review and physical production of labels. Handoff happens 8 weeks before bottling so compliance review has runway.

Operations owns transactional communication (order confirmations, shipping notifications, delivery exceptions). Marketing owns relational communication (release announcements, mailing-list content, post-delivery follow-up). Seam is at the point of order confirmation.

Operations negotiates vendor terms with subject-matter input. Finance reviews the financial substantiation and commitment language before signature. Both departments sign internally before the contract goes to the vendor.

handoffs.artifact: approved label copy draft for TTB/COLA submission
release date, SKU technical sheets, approved-and-printed label text
state tax registration obligation with effective date
production cost actuals and vendor invoice reconciliation
forecasted marketing spend for the month
DTC order projection for fulfillment staffing

owns: brand voice definition and brand guidelines
owned-channel content (email list, Shopify storefront, in-bottle producer notes)
earned-media outreach to wine press, podcasts, trade publications
mailing-list audience development (acquisition, segmentation, retention)
naming for SKUs, collections, and campaigns
producer-voice narrative across all touchpoints
does_not_own: direct-shipping channel setup and state compliance (owned by operations)
distributor and wholesale relationships (owned by operations for logistics, founder-direct for strategic)
customer data storage and CRM technical configuration (owned by operations)
receives_from: operations: release-window confirmations, technical-sheet data, approved label text
finance: allocation sizing for mailing-list release drops, promotional-spend authorization
founder: positioning choices on new SKUs, final approval on paid content
sends_to: operations: approved label copy drafts with 8-week lead time, DTC order projections 30 days before a release
finance: monthly marketing spend forecast and actuals reconciliation
founder: weekly mailing-list growth metrics, quarterly brand-health check
departments.summary: Marketing builds and maintains the audience that buys Quarry Ridge wine through direct-to-consumer channels. Its scope is narrative and audience: brand voice, owned-channel content, press outreach, and mailing-list mechanics. It operates within founder-set positioning (natural-process, place-based, non-luxury) and inside the spend-authorization gates set by finance. Transactional revenue mechanics belong elsewhere; the department's output is brand coherence and audience growth, not order volume.

owns: general ledger, chart of accounts, month-end close
federal tax compliance (TTB excise, IRS corporate, employment)
state tax compliance (PA LCB reporting, state-by-state DTC tax)
cash-flow modeling and runway forecasting
grant applications and compliance reporting
debt-financing applications, loan servicing, covenant compliance
spend authorization gating per delegation thresholds
does_not_own: vendor selection (owned by the department using the vendor)
customer-facing billing content (operations for transactional, marketing for relational)
COGS cost-structure decisions at production (operations owns; finance models)
receives_from: operations: monthly production cost actuals, vendor invoices, facility-fee statements, bonded-warehouse inventory counts
marketing: monthly marketing spend actuals, upcoming campaign budget requests
founder: strategic spend decisions above the gate threshold; financing strategy direction
sends_to: operations: approved-budget signals for procurement, tax substantiation for regulatory filings
marketing: monthly marketing spend variance report, annual marketing budget baseline
founder: weekly cash runway, month-end financial statements and narrative, quarterly P&L and forecast
departments.summary: Finance is the controller function of the company. It owns books and reporting, federal and state tax compliance, grant and debt-financing compliance, and the spend-authorization gating that keeps all departments inside founder-set thresholds. Finance scrutinizes and substantiates rather than decides; commercial and operational decisions belong to the departments with the subject-matter expertise. Its scope is money as it flows through the company: documented, reconciled, and defensible to any external party asking.

owns: vineyard-contract execution and amendment
custom-crush facility scheduling and relationship
harvest operations: pick scheduling, in-bound fruit logistics, weighing, crushing
cellar operations: fermentation monitoring, aging-vessel allocation, racking and topping
dry-goods procurement: glass, closures, labels, shippers, pallets
bonded-warehouse chain of custody and inventory records
federal TTB filings (Basic Permit, ROWPO, COLAs)
state PA Limited Winery License maintenance and LCB reporting
DTC compliance infrastructure: state direct-shipper licenses, ShipCompliant, carrier coordination
fulfillment partner coordination and order routing
does_not_own: strategic vineyard, facility, and market decisions (founder-direct)
marketing content and brand voice (marketing owns)
tax remittance itself (finance owns; operations triggers obligations)
receives_from: founder: strategic direction on vineyards, facility, markets
marketing: approved label copy 8 weeks before bottling, DTC order projections 30 days before release
finance: spend authorization for procurement, tax-status changes across DTC states, approved annual procurement budget
sends_to: marketing: release-window confirmations, technical-sheet data, approved-and-printed labels
marketing: fulfillment status signals during release windows
finance: monthly production cost actuals, vendor invoices, facility-fee statements, inventory counts
finance: tax-obligation triggers per new DTC state opened
founder: weekly production status during harvest and post-harvest, monthly operations dashboard, regulatory-filing status
departments.summary: Operations owns the physical and regulatory flow of wine from vineyard to customer. Vineyard contracts, harvest logistics, cellar operations at the custom-crush facility, bonded-warehouse chain of custody, federal and state regulatory filings, DTC compliance infrastructure, and fulfillment partner coordination. The department executes founder-set strategy rather than setting it. It is the department most exposed to external time windows (harvest, bottling, release dates) and regulatory cadences (TTB, LCB, state DTC registrations).
